assertions.text: Vehicle 1 was heading south in lane two of a two-lane protected turn lane.
The protected turn lane that Vehicle 1 was in was about to merge into a south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane, two-way road.
Vehicle 2 turned right to travel south into lane two of the south-bound road that the protected left turn lanes were merging into.
Vehicle 1 made a right turn across a painted gore before the merge intending to enter a parking lot.
Vehicle 1's right side and Vehicle 2's front end made contact.
Both vehicles came to rest on the road after the collision.
There was one contact between the two vehicles (a single impact).

verdicts.evidence: Telemetry shows vehicle V2 speed first value [6.0 km/h](T2) and last pre-impact [28.0 km/h](T2) in the compute_stats output and time window t_start/t_end in get_window_info, but there is no heading or lane data in the provided channels to confirm travel direction or lane position.

The window info contains no lane-count, map, or merge-geometry information; the EDR summary lists max delta-v values but does not describe road geometry, so the merge-into-three-lane claim is unsupported by sensors.

Speed samples from compute_stats show first [6.0 km/h](T2) and last pre-impact [28.0 km/h](T2), but there is no GPS heading or other vehicle-2 trajectory data in the telemetry to show Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane road.

No heading/GPS/other-vehicle trajectory is present in get_window_info or compute_stats to show Vehicle 2 made the described right turn into lane two; gyroscope/axis alignment is undocumented so turn direction is unverifiable.

Compute_stats shows steering median [172.5 deg](T2) with a peak signed deviation of [−155.0 deg](T2) at [t=-0.5 s] in the steering channel, indicating a large steering input, but road markings and driver intent (enter parking lot) are not recorded so the painted gore/intent claim is unverifiable.

Compute_stats reports cumulative longitudinal delta-V final value [−17.0 km/h](T2) and lateral delta-V peak [4.0 km/h](T2), but device axes are undocumented relative to vehicle frame and there is no direct contact-point data; therefore claims about right-side vs front-end contact are unverifiable.

EDR crash-pulse cumulative delta-V final value is [−17.0 km/h](T2) and the last pre-impact speed sample is [28.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T1), consistent with the vehicle coming to rest after the collision in the recorded crash pulse.

The telemetry shows a single crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaching [−17.0 km/h](T2) at [t=0.19 s](T2) in compute_stats and the window info lists a single EDR record ("Record 1") in get_window_info, consistent with one recorded impact.

result.rationale: V2 telemetry records a single crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V [−17.0 km/h](T2) and pre-impact speeds from [6.0 km/h](T2) to [28.0 km/h](T2), but lacks GPS/heading, lane, multi-vehicle trajectories, and contact-point data required to verify many scene-level assertions.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: ows steering median   with a peak signed deviation of   at [t=-0.5 s] in the steering channel, indicating a large steering inp

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
